behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler wiring: raw-body signature verification plus queue handoff suppression. A pure unit test of only the HMAC function misses real bugs where middleware/body parsing changes bytes before verification. Rejected higher level: no behavior text requires real network/OS/subprocess or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real request parsing/middleware and a test queue adapter. Send a request with intentionally mismatched `X-Signature-256` vs raw body. Assert 401 and assert queue write method was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue). Need real queue persistence path (e.g., local DB/file-backed queue) to catch ack-before-durable-write regressions. Rejected higher level: behavior does not require real external network or subprocess; in-process app plus local persistence is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server + real persistent queue implementation (test DB/filesystem). Instrument timestamps or hooks around queue commit and response send. Assert queue write/commit completes before 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is async decoupling between ingress handler and downstream worker trigger. A single-function unit test can miss integration mistakes where handler accidentally awaits worker promise. Rejected higher level: no real OS/network requirement; can reproduce with in-process modules and controlled worker delay.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler, queue, and a downstream processor stub that blocks on a latch. Send a valid event and assert HTTP response is returned before releasing the latch/completing processing.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Explicit wall-clock SLO under concurrency is sensitive to runtime scheduling, socket handling, and event-loop contention; mocks often hide these latency failures. Real HTTP traffic and real timing are needed. Rejected lower levels: Integration with fakes cannot reliably surface true under-load timing regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process with real HTTP listener. Saturate downstream processing with slow concurrent prior events, then send new requests over real HTTP and measure end-to-end latency. Assert response time stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on interaction between handler logic and persistent queue/idempotency store. Unit logic-only tests miss race/state handoff bugs across modules. Rejected higher level: no requirement for real external network/subprocess; local persistence wiring is enough.
behaviors.test_description: With real queue/idempotency storage in test mode, send two valid requests with same event ID. Assert first is persisted once and second results in no additional queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This checks cross-module error translation (queue full condition -> HTTP status/header contract). Unit-only mapping tests risk missing actual wiring/propagation issues between queue adapter and handler. Rejected higher level: no behavior text requires real network/OS; in-process module wiring captures failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue implementation/config that deterministically returns `full`. Send valid request and assert HTTP 503 plus a valid `Retry-After` header; assert no enqueue success path executed.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component behavior across queue logic + persistence backend under configured capacity. Needs real queue module wired to its storage layer, but not external OS/network primitives beyond local test storage. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real HTTP/subprocess; scope is queue internals with local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against test storage with capacity N. Enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue N+1 and assert explicit rejection/full error and no persisted overflow record.
